author: prdzch
title: Love it
content: Just started building my own flac library from scratch and this has been a lifesaver for listening on my phone. Would love to see a change font size feature because it cuts off some of the artists name with … and kinda looks ugly :/

title: Paid version contains ads
content: The paid version still shows ads, often some which are inappropriate . I thought the Apple App Store was safer than the google play store

author: Nick___M
title: Baffling Interface
content: The application does what I need it to do, but the interface is extremely confusing.

author: AaData
title: Ads
content: I don’t want ads.  Sorry.

author: Fusion14N
title: 70 dollars for a music app...?
content: For 70 dollars this needs to do everything apple music does but better, and wash my dishes and iron your clothes for you. It doesn't it's quite buggy on older devices and has trouble playing music when switching apps or when the screen is locked.

author: Minthony Stone
title: Great app
content: This is a great music player for local files!! I like that it has a customizable player interface and no ads interrupting my music. It's got a bit of a learning curve, but other than that, I haven't had any issues so far.

author: 314.mp4
title: Great and easy to use app
content: I’ve switched off of Spotify lately because I really don’t support what they’re doing with AI, and their subscription is extremely expensive. This app plays everything flawlessly and I think the premium is really worth it; I especially appreciate that there’s an option for a one time purchase rather than limiting you to a subscription. Great app and thanks to the developers for updates :)

author: MaxKairi91
content: I almost loved this app. If it only had an option when clicking the 3 dots it'll go to the album that you’re listening to instead of searching for the song from the album!

author: terrishar
title: Simple way to listen to musics.
content: I have found Flacbox to be the best so far. All music files can be imported. No need to converse music I've downloaded. It's simple and hassle-free. Thank you.

author: Wackydoc
title: Very capable
content:
It has many features, but it is taking me a little bit to learn the menu options.
Still it is very versatile. Geared toward streaming, which I don’t subscribe to.

author: johnjohn_xyz
title: 20 or more frequency band equalizer up to 20,000hz pls?🥲🫶
content: 2nd review of this amazing app the only app on apple store that we can truely rely on to play lossless audio, not perfect but i think in time theyll be fixed crashes a lot i noticed when handling big lossless files but for lossy its smooth, it would be amzing if we can have 20 or more frequency band equalizer on the app🥲🫶 a little fun equalizer for iphone16promax speaker: set the eq to: 32=-12 64=-9 125=2.6 250=-4 500=11 1k=4 2k=-7 4k=-6 8k=-11 16k=11, (i removed the microgrill on the speaker to let the higher frequecy and 250hz go out better), then play lossless audio or lossless/lossy dolby atmos songs on the speaker,if you know where to get it, and viola you now have a handheld lossless/dolby atmos virtual in your hand!! play music and blow people away that sound is coming from an iphone😂, (this could alsowork for 17,15,14promax) note: volume will decrease a little bit but it will be flat or use the db+ if its not loud enoud. Muah

author: Secure Guy
title: Keeps turning off randomly
content: Unable to use; keeps turning off randomly.

author: alissa914
title: Supports a lot of formats but never seems to offline download anything from Dropbox or SMB
content:
Well it seems to work albeit a bit slowly. I couldn’t get it to work with Dropbox though. When it syncs, it seems to work but it takes a long time. I mean…. Any sync action takes forever but I’m starting to think this is more an issue with iOS just not allowing it to run in the background to do all this. But even so, just to load a directory from the cloud took hours and accomplished very little. So I just got a flash drive, put all my music from Dropbox on it, and used Files to put it all on my phone. Works well now. So it’s a great player but don’t buy it for the cloud sync. It doesn’t seem worth it for that but most blame likely goes to iOS there.

Edit 2:  After having it play music for a few days and setting up the Dropbox sync, it synchronized everything. Guess it was the lack of background time it would get. But after it fully synched up, I used it in my CarPlay and it just sounds incredible. Really does. Only gripe is that I can’t find volume leveling but if it’s there, I will find it. If not, I’d highly request that it gets added. Sound quality makes me no longer want iTunes on this phone or Apple Music. This sounds so much better. 

Edit:  seems I can see one problem and it’s that the app can’t be set to run in the background unless it’s actively playing music. iOS seems to put it to sleep so background tasks appear to pause even if plugged in.  Coming from Windows Phone OS, we used to see this a lot there when msft would let its apps do maintenance tasks but no one else could.  I’m guessing it’s a power management thing.  Like here, I copied all the files to my phone directly and have it doing metadata reads. It pauses unless you’re on that settings screen and it’s playing music. Again, seems more like an OS thing which I hope the dev can get permission to allow it to run unpaused in the background as a dev option, but it seems not to be the app problem otherwise. Just a consequence of being limited by Apple

author: Josh.yu
title: Constant Crashes After Subscription Purchase
content: Flacbox used to work well for my setup, but after buying the annual subscription and listening a few times, it now crashes constantly. I’m playing songs stored on my Synology NAS with a DAC attached to my iPhone, and the app often quits after a short period of playback. This makes it very hard to enjoy music and reliably use the app with my existing library and hardware. I hope the developer can fix these stability issues quickly so the app becomes usable again.

author: DXT2000
title: Now has full screen ads
content: Used to great but now has full screen ads that prevents you from interacting with the app. There are also ads displayed on the album art. Also uses monthly subscription instead of single time purchase…

author: Richard Station
title: The music player that I’m looking for
content: I’m looking for a music player that also you can use equalizer, pitch tones, etc, and I found it. Thanks.

author: Idk brot
title: Lost song
content: I lost my song trying to recover them by files to move to a carpet I couldn’t because this ap was using it

author: Daminiquewinner
title: X button
content: X button not working to clear the previous typed name. Nov 16 update

author: CaritaDeAngel8
title: Love the EQ
content: I use this along the STEMZ app. Remove the vocals guitars and other instruments or just slightly lower them, then transfer those files to this app and boost the bass and walah the bass is even better especially because the eq toggles are fully adjustable to my desired preference. I use to dream of apps like this back in 2012 but they lacked, now the future is here

author: Dawginexile
title: ‼️Crashes, Developer Unresponsive, Requesting a Refund 💸
content:
I liked this app—when it actually worked. All of a sudden it just quits after a couple of seconds. And I see I’m not the only one having this problem check the other reviews Reddit and Twitter as well. 

I tried all the usual methods to resolve a problem like this. Hard quitting the app, restarting the phone, deleting, and re-downloading the app and NOTHING WORKS. 

Because I hate ridiculous and negative reviews from lazy people, I first tried to contact the developer to resolve the issue. I reached out via email and got no response. I messaged the developer on Reddit and got no response. And then I tried DM the developer on Twitter and got no response. So this is my only option—to leave a one star review that should be a zero star review. 

If the developer pays attention to these reviews at all: I want to request a refund. The app literally does not work.

author: sus bush
title: Very Solid 😊
content: Like the app a lot, and use it everyday. However, I wish the free version had more customization available. Also would be pretty cool if you could sort how albums and playlists show up manually, instead of by different categories (Think dragging them around so you can keep favorites at the top)

author: BubGames
title: ⚠️ CRASHES ⚠️
content:
THE APP STILL CRASHES AFTER A BIT OF PLAYING MUSIC.

It used to be way worse. I used to play one song and it wouldn't even finish before the app crashes. After it would crash though it wouldn't crash until you dont use the app for a while or restart it again. Now I can play around 10 songs but the app still crashes. Pls try to fix I know its hard but this is a crucial bug and its very annoying

author: AncientMystic
title: Forcing users to allow data collection unless you pay
content: This app forces users to allow data collection in new versions and treats disabling it as a premium feature. So you have to pay to not submit your data to google, which is borderline illegal under GDPR. Uninstalled. “Freely Given Consent (Article 7(4) and Recital 43) The core issue lies in Article 7(4) of the GDPR, which states: "When assessing whether consent is freely given, utmost account shall be taken of whether... the performance of a contract is made conditional on consent to processing personal data that is not necessary for the performance of that contract." This is reinforced by Recital 43, which clarifies that consent is not freely given if the user has no genuine or free choice or is unable to refuse or withdraw consent without detriment”

author: M.Ghiasi
title: Excellent
content: This program is amazing and I love it

author: Legacy VK
title: No Lock Screen Playback
content:
Overall I truly believe this is one of the top greatest music apps of all time. In fact, I don’t have anything negative to say, I think all features work perfectly and to have the endless possibilities of customizing and so much more is another huge plus. I simply would LOVE Lock Screen control as I’m sure others would too. 

Using Apple Music, you can pause, play, skip or go back a song. I would like to be able to do this with Flacbox and then this will be the number 1 music app of all time. Please and thank you 🙏🏻

author: E3257
title: Pretty Good
content: If you’re looking for something relatively quick and dirty, this is very useful. Not to say that it’s poor quality, and it provides a lot for free. You can compose a playlist just fine, even for my advanced need, which was to compose a playlist of my own songs with poor encryption! However, a lot of encrypted data can get lost, so if you want lyrics to show up properly, I’m afraid you’re at a bit of a loss. Good product, though. 👍

author: Mitsukoflac
title: More Equalizer band pls? 🥹🫶 we love this app!!
content: Best player for hifi audio period, it would be amazing if we have 20 or more band equalizer? I travel alot i can only have my phone with me and i play a lot of HI-FI music on the go, i have more bands on the laptop but im not bringing that on vacation

content: It feels like the ads are interfering with the files that you are listening to. After some time the program loses the connection with the file you try to listen, so you loose the progress and have to re-open the file.

author: tib777777777777777777
title: Amazing Music App!
content: I love all the features it has and can even import playlists from file. Flac and other filetypes supported. Amazing app! Well worth the money for premium

author: Anonymous App Revlewer
title: Just use another app
content:
The moment a full screen ad shows making me wait 10-30 seconds before being able to access the player is the moment I’m switching away. I don’t care that you tried to get me to keep this app by hiding ads for a few days before showing them, I play local because of ads and offline playback.

If you’re looking for a local player, Foobar2000 is good. No ads, no hidden paywalls, just a music app

If you want this app, make sure in the subscription menu you go to the options menu to find the one time $30 purchase instead of the $10/Year

title: Not syncing from Synology

author: In fin it

content: I use this as a basic MP3 player because I hate syncing my music with the Apple app, and have used it for years. They didn’t used to have ads, instead relying on charging for more features. They’ve started putting ads in the app which isn’t a problem when they’re part of the app screen, but they’re now putting up whole video ads with no way to obviously close them down in the scummy practice of tricking you into a redirect. I’m looking for another basic MP3 player now.

title: Not working
content: Is not working with WD My Cloud hard drive

content: Guy works hard. Don't need a Cloud, Windows, or Mac to get stuff on here.

author: CMaker3
title: Great music app with so many features
content: I’ve owned this app for a long time but have only discovered so many features that I didn’t know exited until I needed them. And bham! It made my day.

author: Artoman
title: Not as before
content:
Раньше это был мой один из любимейших плейеров. Самые значимые для меня альбомы были помещены именно в этот плейер. Сейчас столько бесплатных flac плейеров, включая apple player, что кажется даже неприлично просить $30 за Flac Player. Похоже на лебединую песню! - снять на последок. А жаль…
Придется все отсюда удалять, и перепрофилировать свою дискографию

author: MaeLitroX
title: Good, but a few things
content:
Overall the app is really nice. Most apps don’t have custom speed control (especially without the pitch correction) so seeing it here was really nice. However, this app does lack things like Gapless Playback, and will instead skip the last second of a song or wait a bit, depending on the codec you’re using. Music controls also have a bit of delay, whether it be you’re skipping or replaying the song.

I also feel like the lifetime price is a tad bit overpriced, but that’s just my personal opinion. I do like the fact that ads don’t stop your music so I may just be prying too much lol.

Now my last thing, which is a personal preference: No playback count. I personally like seeing what songs/artists I’ve listened to much, so seeing how this wasn’t a thing that some other apps had made me a bit disappointed.

I’ll stick with it until I see something better though, much love <3

author: That Tuning Guy
title: Great Music Player!
content: I’ve been using this player for several years now and it’s never let me down. I discovered today that I can even play my music from my iCloud Storage without downloading it.

author: Pogodian
title: Study All Settings
content: This is a great app, but maybe not for people who want a fully automatic and transparent program. Take the time to carefully adjust your settings and you will be rewarded. There is fantastic file management and sync with total control over details and devices. However, adjusting the settings is very easy and totally comprehensible to anyone who understands basic file management. Highly recommended app for all but perhaps the computer illiterate.

author: Ghostemaney
title: Legendary. A must have for Audiophiles.
content:
It is an exceptional application specifically designed to enhance your music experience. 

It allows you to fine-tune your music with its meticulous but easy-to-use E.Q. Interface, which has all the frequency options you need to make any genre of music sound lively and crystal clear.

Props to the developers for making such a fantastic app! Bravo!

author: Nasty Britches
title: Update pushed
content: Over an extended period, I have been utilizing the application. Recently, an update was released, necessitating the discontinuation of my previous version. Consequently, I am now prohibited from utilizing features that were previously accessible. This update was not requested by me and was not a part of my intended usage. The application’s author should be held accountable for employing such marketing tactics.

author: Meweaa
title: Great value
content: I’ve had this app for at least three or four years and many many more however, this one stands out as the improvement just keep coming for an old man. I can’t hear much. This app is as good as any pair of hearing aids I’ve ever owned. Keep up the good work.

author: Banjilawal
title: It's not great duplicates files
content: You have to tell the application where your music files are every time you add a new one then it makes its own copy wasting space.  The ads are difficult to skip past. It's always trying to get me to install some third party app I don't need

author: badprogger
title: Perfect for Hi-Res Audio and Cloud Streaming!
content: Flacbox is an absolute must-have for anyone serious about their music. The app supports all the high-res audio formats I use, like FLAC and ALAC, and it plays them perfectly. It’s so convenient to stream directly from my cloud storage—Google Drive, Dropbox, and even my NAS are all supported. This means I can access my entire high-quality music collection anytime, anywhere, without cluttering up my phone's storage. The offline mode is another lifesaver for when I’m traveling. I can download the tracks I want and listen without needing internet access. I also love the playlist management. It’s super easy to create and organize playlists, and the ability to set audio bookmarks is a great touch. It helps me keep track of specific parts in longer mixes or podcasts. One of the best things about Flacbox is that it can open almost any audio file format. No matter what file I throw at it, the app plays it smoothly without any hassle. If you care about high-quality audio and need a flexible player that works with cloud services, Flacbox is unbeatable!

author: thefourthcolour
title: Two issues
content: I have been using this app for my music projects recently, and I love it and think it is much superior to other similar programs, but there are two pretty big issues that are getting me. One: I have a lot of songs that "segue", or bleed into each other and are directly connected, and this app takes a few seconds to apparently "load" the next song. On Apple Music and other programs, seamless segues are allowed, but on Flacbox, there is a pause, which screws up the listening experience. Two: If I am inactive on the app for a day or so, my entire music library erases, and I have to reupload everything from my cloud drive. As soon as it is there, it reattaches the audio tags and assigns songs to their artists and albums, but it takes at least 15 minutes. Also, when I try to use the app on my phone, under the same account, I have to upload my entire music library, again, and wait for all the albums to be assigned. I haven't figured out a way to stop this so far. Both issues are really annoying, but otherwise the app works well. Please pay attention to this review, as I have reviewed the app multiple times stating the same issues, and I can't find a way to email/contact the creator. Thanks

author: HawaiiMaui
title: EQ works on iPad, SMB2 Win10 sharing
content:
I can connect to file shares on my Win10 PC. This is amazing, since I WFH I can use my iPad to play MP3's or FLAC and not need to download files and use up my precious (paltry) 32GB of storage. [Any other app that claims SMB but hasn't been updated since 2018 won't let you authenticate with Windows. MS disabled SMB1 and the devs needed to update to SMB2 or SMB3; this app has kept up to date.]

This app also has an EQ, this basically lets me use any halfway decent earphones and make them sound much better. Buying this app with EQ was much cheaper than buying high end wireless earphones. I've used EQ's for years with speakers to neutralize the room acoustics and love them with headphones too. The EQ sliders were missing on iPad but a few emails with the dev and they've got it working in the Dec 2020 build.

The SMB (cloud) playback also seems less buggy, or my Wi-Fi is behaving. The app was pretty unforgiving of bad network connections, but this one hasn't crashed for hours. (Their MP3 EQ app has 3x retry options in the settings but the FLAC one doesn't.)

author: jaa2023
title: Tried again and love it!
content:
A little less than a year ago I started trying to stream my own (mostly high rez) music collection.  I tried several of the obvious choices in the App Store, including Flacbox.  It was all pretty frustrating and I gave up on it.  Too much effort for buggy software, poor synchronization, missing songs, etc. 

Next I tried Roon and Roon Arc.  That showed promise at first - but had much of the same problems only at a very high price point.  I gave up on that too (I use an Aurender in my main (old fashioned 2 big speaker) system.  

A week or two ago I decided to try a few App Store solutions again to see if they might have become more viable.  Flacbox was the clear winner and I’ve been working with it on both my iPhone and my iPad.  I am thrilled with Flacbox v4.2!  Look, nothing in this space is perfect yet.  You need some patience and some time to re-download, re-sync, change song orders (with a great sort feature), etc.  But now I have 125gb of uncompressed music on both my Apple devices and can use the online files (synced to my Google Drive) to replace downloads whenever I want (I haven’t streamed my online files much yet).  Lastly, the Flacbox album display is beautiful. 

 I highly recommend Flacbox and now just hope the app developer has a sustainable business model and keeps this app and future versions running like v4.2 does now.

author: Mohawke
title: Best music player
content:
As a Linux user this is really the only way to use my music collection with my iPhone. I set up SMB and pull songs down and build the playlists in Flacbox. Been doing that for years but it was recently broke.

In a world of monthly fees just to listen to music with a ton of overly feature packed garbage players, also asking for monthly fees and your email address, so you can listen offline without draining your cell plans usage or simply grabbing music from the Apple player that works the same way this was awesome.

The only issue? Lately when I add songs via SMB, even after waiting for them to download, some are missing and no amount of attempts to get them downloaded works. Fix that and I’ll give it five stars. The http process works fine but it’s tedious. I really hope this player sticks around or I’ll have write my own and use TestFlight, because I hate customers and don’t want to pay dev fees and own other Apple garbage products to produce one piece of software.

author: IDontNeedNoStupidNickname
title: So much better than native Apple app
content:
I really like Flacbox on my iPhone. With all my music stored in the cloud (in my case OneDrive), Flacbox basically gives me my own, personalized streaming service. No need to use iTunes to move a few songs here or there. No Apple tricks (like substituting the version of a song in THEIR library for the one I’m trying to download, then telling me I don’t have the rights to play it). Flacbox has excellent sound quality AND a full-featured equalizer accessible from within the app (no more switching out to Settings and selecting from an inane list of presets to compensate for the Apple player’s crappy sound).  And Flacbox, unlike Apple, isn’t constantly trying to SELL me something. 

There is a learning curve, but it’s worth it. Are there a few glitches? Sure. But the developer seems committed to improvements and fixes. They’re a small player competing on a field of giants. Respect!

author: Walker300E
title: Great App!!
content: Love it. You can tag your files in the app, and everything. Lossless and all! Only thing is I wish that you could drag songs quicker when rearranging your playlists into a certain order other than alphabetical etc. because when you have so many songs it takes DAYS to make it right. Also one last thing is I wish you could change the text size to make it smaller and even change change the shape of your covers to be just a normal square with the edges. Most cover arts have the parental advisory in the bottom corners, and it gets cut off in this app. Other than that, 0 complaints. The app started randomly crashing a couple days ago, but I just uninstalled and reinstalled the app. Reset everything sadly, but no crashing. Maybe add an account feature where it saves playlists and stuff? Thank you for this amazing app! <3

author: That1HoneyBadger
title: Almost perfect!
content:
This is a necessity for anyone who gets their music from other places other than streaming services.  A lot of great artists don't have everything up on streaming services -- deep cuts, rarities, etc.  So having a solid Audio Player is a must.  Flacbox is the best Ive come across, and its at a reasonable price point, unlike the other very excellent (but deeply overpriced subscription based Audio Player).

One thing I would like to see, is a bit of polish in the UI.  Its good, but its not great.  I find that the navigation makes for a less than smooth UX.  Being able to have Music, Artist, Album, Song on the bottom level would be nice, or maybe being able to customize the bottom level would be nice.  Also, I think a bit more elegance in the interface is in order, something akin to the Sony Walkman (Or maybe even implementing skins)!

Otherwise, fantastic app -- a permanent app of my iPhone!  Keep it up!

author: Super Cheese
title: Everything you would want in a player
content: I use this app to listen to podcasts and sermons that I want to have locally but not in my iTunes library. Great for managing files as it has robust cloud service interface.  Since I study the material bookmark feature is awesome and works well with apple watch so you can bookmark while walking and listening.  My only gripe is that I wish the developer would fix the lock screen controls to rewind and bookmark. As opposed to bookmark and fast forward. Would be five star if this change was made. It is a little annoying that is change or improvement has not been implemented especially because I have been asking for it for over half a year, and the developer takes pride in consistently updating the app. Please implement this change.
